title: psydrax odoratus subsp. australianus
intro: Psydrax odoratus subsp. australianus is a subspecies of the Psydrax odoratus plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is commonly known as the 'Australian Fringed Violet' and is native to Australia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Psydrax odoratus subsp. australianus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
